title: Inert Prime Area Quantization FAIL: p=3, k=2 — forbidden set claims 9 is absent (FORBIDDEN_SET_MISMATCH)
_fixture_purpose: Demonstrates FORBIDDEN_SET_MISMATCH: the cert claims forbidden={3,6,9} for p=3,k=2 (modulus=9), but 9≡0 is in the spectrum (f(0,0)=0=9 mod 9). The value 9 mod 9 is 0, which has v_3(0)=∞ (not 1), so 0 IS achievable. The cert incorrectly includes 0 (alias 9 in the range {1..9}) in the forbidden set, misrepresenting the theorem. The actual forbidden set is {3,6} only. This is the canonical forbidden-set obstruction certificate for QA_AREA_QUANTIZATION_PK_CERT.v1.

inert_witness.notes: p=3 is inert. Correct. The error is in the forbidden set claim.

theorem_claim.statement: WRONG CLAIM: forbidden = {3,6,9}. Correct claim is {3,6}.
theorem_claim.forbidden_description: WRONG: claims 9 is absent. But 9 mod 9 = 0, and 0 = f(0,0) is always achieved. v_3(0) = infinity, not 1 — so 0 is not in the predicted forbidden set. The forbidden set should only contain {3,6}.

validation_checks.description: p=3 is inert in Z[phi]: Legendre(5,3)=-1

validation_checks.details: actual_spectrum={0,1,2,4,5,7,8} == claimed spectrum (spectrum claim is correct; only the forbidden claim is wrong)

claimed_forbidden={3,6,9} but theorem_forbidden={3,6}; 9 mod 9 = 0 has v_3(0)=inf (not 1) and IS achievable. Spurious entry: {9}. This also means claimed_forbidden is not a subset of {0..8} (9 is out of range); the certificate over-counts.

fail_ledger.message: claimed forbidden={3,6,9} does not match theorem prediction {r in {0..8} : v_3(r)=1}={3,6}. The element 9 is out of range (modulus=9 means residues are {0..8}; 9 mod 9 = 0) and v_3(0)=inf ≠ 1. The correct forbidden set is {3,6}.
